verdict: The North Star's "answer it from the screen" bar has no maintained home. The one comprehensive player-facing spec (UI/UX v4.1, CANONICAL 2026-04-16) predates the entire current generation, is absent from CURRENT.md, and its 20-P1 repair workplan never executed — so the only current-era player-interaction artifact is the contest walkthrough (DRAFT, one subsystem). Applying the three dramatic-legibility questions per scale: only Agôn contest passes; combat fails outright (no per-exchange input, stakeless Wounds); governance, faction, thread, and season-close are partial-to-weak. The player must hold ~27-30 named trackers across scales — far above the corpus's own immersion ceilings (3-4 personal / 7 strategic) — with presentation-layer translation existing for only two scales.
key_points: Contest walkthrough is the only CURRENT-ERA player-interaction model; a large CANONICAL UI/UX v4.1 exists but is stale (2026-04-16, pre-d+σ/combat_engine_v1/contest-rebuild/Key-substrate), absent from CURRENT.md, and its v4.2 replacement was never built.

The stale v4.1 actively contaminates the Godot-facing reference: it still specifies Taint (0-6) and CD as player-facing tracks that canon STRUCK (threadwork_v30 line 653/984/990); the repair items are untracked and their proposed ED numbers are already occupied.
~27-30 named trackers across scales vs the 2026-05-08 immersion audit's own ceilings (3-4 personal, 7 strategic); only contest and (partially) combat carry any presentation-layer translation.

Dramatic-legibility walk: Agôn contest PASS; faction strategic PARTIAL (whose-at-risk scattered, GM-controls-votes hole); governance WEAK (no NPC ambition field); combat FAIL (no per-exchange input, stakeless Wounds); thread PARTIAL (MS/RS split blocks one-sentence read); season-close UNEVEN (articulation blind to thread/era, starvation gap).
~6 of 7 scales have no live player-interaction model and no index entry commissioning one — the GM-removal mandate the walkthrough exists to satisfy is unmet almost everywhere.
Preserve: the walkthrough's resolved-value=visible-value + hidden-value->consequence-class contract, and v4.1's capability-gated method (salvage, not rewrite).

findings.claim: The only comprehensive player-facing spec (UI/UX v4.1, CANONICAL) predates the entire current generation, is absent from CURRENT.md, and its 20-P1 repair workplan (v4.2) was never executed — leaving the 'from the screen' North Star bar with no maintained home and the DRAFT one-subsystem contest walkthrough as the only current-era interaction artifact.
findings.cite: designs/ui/valoria_ui_ux_v4_1.md header (CANONICAL, 2026-04-16, deps='19 v30 docs'+combat_v30); CURRENT.md (no designs/ui or player_agency row); designs/ui/valoria_ui_ux_v4_2_workplan.md (69 findings/20 P1, target v4.2 absent from dir)

findings.claim: Only Agôn contest has a current-era player-interaction model; combat, domain/settlement governance, faction strategic, thread ops, mass battle, and season-close/articulation have none and no live index entry commissioning one, so the GM-removal mandate the walkthrough exists to satisfy is unmet for ~6 of 7 scales.
findings.cite: designs/audit/2026-07-01-contest-player-interaction/player_interaction_walkthrough_v1.md (scoped to 'the one game fully built'); CURRENT.md (no player-interaction rows for other scales)

findings.claim: The player must hold ~27-30 named trackers across scales (Coherence/Fatigue/Face/Concentration/Persuasion/Doubt/Wounds/Momentum/Resources/TS/Certainty/Evidence/Exposure/Disposition/Standing/Renown + Mandate/Stability/Accord/Turmoil/CI/MS-RS/IP/Prosperity-Order-Defense/L-PS/Treasury), far above the corpus's own immersion ceilings, with presentation-layer translation existing for only two scales.
findings.cite: designs/audit/2026-05-08-meta-audit-immersion.md §3,§5 (3-4 personal / 7 strategic ceiling; mechanical vocabulary leak); designs/architecture/player_agency_v30.md §5-§6 (Standing/Renown/scene-budget)
findings.tracked_ref: 2026-05-08-meta-audit-immersion §5 finding 2 (new scope: cross-scale count + stale mitigating layer)

findings.claim: The CANONICAL Godot-facing UI reference still specifies Taint (0-6) and CD as player-facing tracks that canon explicitly struck, so a developer building the character sheet renders two trackers that do not exist; the repair items are untracked and their proposed ED numbers are already occupied by unrelated entries.
findings.cite: designs/ui/valoria_ui_ux_v4_1.md §9.7/§9.8/§10.2/§11.3; designs/threadwork/threadwork_v30.md:653,984,990 (Taint struck); designs/ui/valoria_ui_ux_v4_2_workplan.md F-15/F-16; canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl ED-638/639/641 (occupied)

findings.claim: player_agency routes a scene into combat as the player's choice of system, but the engagement loop exposes no per-exchange player-decision parameter and Health/Wounds carry no goal/stake/season-clock, so all three dramatic-legibility questions fail from the combat screen — the one current-era scale with neither an interaction model nor threadwork.
findings.cite: designs/architecture/player_agency_v30.md §4.4 (scene->combat routing); dossier personal_combat (engagement() no player-decision param; scene.combat_resolved health-bar payload); designs/audit/.../00_grounding/03_threadwork_surface.md §3 (ED-911)
findings.tracked_ref: ED-911 (thread gap); combat legibility per personal_combat dossier pc-legibility-fails

findings.claim: Season-close narrative surfacing runs through articulation, which is silent on Thread/Calamity, self-contradictory on battle-conclusion coverage, blind to victory-era transitions, and has a starvation path (a Bonded NPC whose Keys never match a trigger is never rendered) — so 'what happened / whose position moved' is weakest exactly at the moment designed to answer it.
findings.cite: dossier articulation (starvation-mechanism-unwired §3.3/§5; thread-silence full-text; ED-1006 era-blindness); designs/audit/.../00_grounding/03_threadwork_surface.md §3

findings.claim: A player cannot state in one sentence which world track is failing because the MS-vs-RS naming split is live across canonical docs, imposing a legibility tax on the corpus's richest decision space.
findings.cite: designs/audit/.../00_grounding/03_threadwork_surface.md §1 (MS in threadwork_v30 §5 vs RS in params/threadwork.md); dossier threadwork ms-rs-split-live (ED-731/772 sweep never reached params)
